page: Amazon Web Services d2.2xlarge is a dense storage instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2676 v3 processor running at 2.4 GHz. Built on the Xen hypervisor, this x86_64 architecture server includes 61.0 GB of system memory, yielding 7.62 GB per core. The defining hardware characteristic is its massive local storage capacity, offering 12,288 GB of local HDD storage. Network connectivity is provisioned with a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate that the d2.2xlarge is optimized for storage capacity rather than raw compute speed. The Intel Xeon E5-2676 v3 CPU delivers single-core performance in the bottom 10% and multi-core performance in the bottom 25% of tested servers. Memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks is average, though write speeds and small cached block reads fall into the bottom 25%. Cryptographic and compression tests, such as gzip and bzip3, also rank in the lower quartiles, reflecting the older processor generation.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the d2.2xlarge offers high resource density specifically for local storage capacity. It provides a massive storage-to-compute ratio, making it highly cost-effective for workloads requiring large-scale data retention without demanding high-performance IOPS or modern CPU instruction sets like AVX-512. However, the tradeoff is clear: compute-heavy tasks, high-speed database transactions, and real-time processing will be bottlenecked by the legacy CPU architecture and HDD-based storage medium.

This instance is best suited for storage-intensive workloads that do not require high random I/O performance. Appropriate use cases include MapReduce processing, log analysis, data warehousing, and large-scale file systems. It is also suitable for backup and archiving tasks where sequential read and write operations on large datasets are the primary requirement.
description: Amazon Web Services d2.2xlarge is a dense storage instance designed for workloads requiring massive local capacity. It features 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2676 v3 processor running at 2.4 GHz, paired with 61.0 GB of RAM and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. The server's primary feature is its 12,288 GB of local HDD storage. Benchmark data shows that compute and memory write performances are generally weak, with single-core CPU performance in the bottom 10% and multi-core in the bottom 25%. Consequently, this instance is not intended for high-performance computing or intensive database transactions. Instead, it offers qualitative cost efficiency for sequential storage-heavy operations. The d2.2xlarge is best suited for MapReduce, log processing, data warehousing, and large-scale archiving workloads where storage volume is prioritized over raw processing speed.
og_description: Amazon Web Services d2.2xlarge is a dense storage instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 61.0 GB of RAM, and 12,288 GB of local HDD storage, optimized for large-scale sequential data workloads and archiving.

tagline: A dense storage instance offering massive local HDD capacity and balanced memory for sequential data processing and archiving workloads.

8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores via Intel Xeon E5-2676 v3.
61.0 GB of system memory providing 7.62 GB per core.
Best suited for MapReduce, log analysis, and data warehousing.
Optimized for sequential storage-heavy tasks rather than high-compute workloads.
